description_long: This obelisk is a tall, slender monument with a rectangular cross-section and a rounded top. The surface appears to be made of a uniform, light gray stone, with the front face (view 3) featuring a series of vertical grooves or fluting that run the length of the structure, adding texture and visual interest. The sides are smooth and flat, while the back is also smooth and unadorned. The overall form is monolithic and imposing, typical of commemorative or decorative obelisks found in urban parks or plazas. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative motifs aside from the vertical grooves on the front. The base is not distinctly separated from the shaft, giving the impression of a continuous, uninterrupted form.
description: A tall, slender gray stone obelisk with a rounded top and vertical grooves on the front face. The sides and back are smooth and unadorned.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile and smooth, flat surface of the obelisk.
description_view_1: A rear view displaying a broad, smooth, and unadorned surface with a rounded top.
description_view_2: Another narrow side view, similar to view 0, showing the thin profile and smooth surface.
description_view_3: The front view reveals vertical grooves running the length of the obelisk, with a rounded top and a textured appearance.
attribute_ornamentation: Vertical grooves on the front face; no other ornamentation visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is integrated with the shaft, rectangular in shape, and lacks any distinct platform, steps, or decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is smoothly rounded with no additional decoration or unique features.
